title: Federal Reserve Board announces it has made the joint findings with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency required for the OCC to approve a request by Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A., for an exemption under section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act
summary: The Federal Reserve Board announced on March 26, 2026, that it has made the joint findings with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) required for the OCC to approve Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A.'s request for an exemption under section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act. The exemption is needed to facilitate an internal corporate reorganization involving Morgan Stanley Europe SE in Frankfurt, Germany.
key_points: Federal Reserve Board and OCC jointly made findings required for OCC approval of Morgan Stanley Bank's section 23A exemption request
Section 23A establishes limits and imposes requirements on a bank's transactions with its affiliates
The exemption request is for an internal corporate reorganization involving Morgan Stanley Europe SE, Frankfurt am Main, Germany
Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A. is headquartered in Salt Lake City, Utah
Press release references an Order (PDF) and statements from Vice Chair Jefferson, Vice Chair Bowman, and Governors Barr and Cook
why_it_matters: This is a specific regulatory action approving a section 23A exemption for Morgan Stanley Bank to facilitate an internal corporate reorganization involving its affiliate Morgan Stanley Europe SE. The source provides no details on the reorganization specifics, any associated risks, or broader market implications. Section 23A exemptions affect how banks structure affiliate transactions, but this appears to be a routine administrative approval with no discussed policy significance or market-moving potential.
